mechanism_narrative: Positive retail-sales momentum signals resilient household cash-flow growth and stronger near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS growth is persistent.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest available value at decision time was 448086
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day return spread versus lower-growth observations, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in FEDFUNDS from the zero-bound tightening regime increases discount rates and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the policy-rate increase is observable.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.10pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value at decision time was 0.24
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and deteriorating household income prospects, leading analysts to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.30pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest available value at decision time was 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns after triggered UNRATE increases are not lower than non-triggered periods, or if the negative hit rate is below 55% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction while payroll employment remains high signals a margin squeeze in which output demand weakens before labor costs adjust, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls over 3 months while PAYEMS rises over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
series:PAYEMS
fact:INDPRO latest available value at decision time was 106.0222
fact:PAYEMS latest available value at decision time was 143242
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-down and PAYEMS-up trigger does not underperform periods with both series rising, or if the interaction term has a nonnegative coefficient in walk-forward predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI together with rising FEDFUNDS signals that inflation pressure is forcing tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.05pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value at decision time was 238.111
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value at decision time was 0.24
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-up interaction is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if it fails to underperform CPIAUCSL-up but FEDFUNDS-flat cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in housing starts signals stronger residential investment, credit creation, and durable-goods demand, causing positive revisions to cyclical earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest available value at decision time was 1173.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST increases do not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the return spread is negative over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment acceleration signals stronger aggregate income and near-term sales growth without requiring an immediate recession-risk repricing, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds its prior 12-month average change
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
fact:PAYEMS latest available value at decision time was 143242
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS acceleration has a nonpositive 5-day return spread versus PAYEMS deceleration in walk-forward tests, or if the signal hit rate falls below 50% after transaction-free directional scoring.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth combined with soft retail sales signals broad demand deceleration that lowers expected revenue growth and raises recession-risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is below 0.5% annualized and RSAFS falls over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:RSAFS
fact:GDPC1 latest available value at decision time was 16414.0
fact:RSAFS latest available value at decision time was 448086
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-GDPC1 and falling-RSAFS trigger is not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of out-of-sample triggered observations.
